● Bash(git log --oneline --since="2026-02-28" --all | grep -iE "launch|workflow|one-shot|prompt|modal|tui" | head -30) ⎿ e0d9c6931 Trigger CI workflows 1666fac19 Trigger CI workflows 1dde75c8c Trigger CI workflows … +27 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
● Let me now explore the key files to understand what incremental dispatch actually delivered and how it overlaps with the objective's remaining work.
● Explore(Explore incremental dispatch vs objective) ⎿ Done (33 tool uses · 63.9k tokens · 1m 4s) (ctrl+o to expand)
❯ Tool loaded.
● Let me check a few more things to understand the full picture — the current state of PR #8593 and what the one-shot workflow looks like today.
● Bash(gh pr view 8593 --json body,createdAt,updatedAt --repo dagster-io/erk --jq '{created: .createdAt, updated: .updatedAt, body_length: (.body | length)}') ⎿ { "body_length": 10258, "created": "2026-03-02T02:08:39Z", … +2 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
● Searched for 1 pattern, read 2 files (ctrl+o to expand)
● Let me check the current launch_cmd.py handler and the incremental-dispatch command.
● Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)
● Now let me read the dispatch handler to see what modify_existing: "true" does currently.
● Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)
● Now I have a complete picture. Let me write the evaluation.
● Updated plan ⎿ /plan to preview
● Here's the summary of what I found:
The objective is partially superseded. Incremental dispatch (/erk:pr-incremental-dispatch, landed via #8785 + follow-up fixes) covers the core capability — dispatching AI work against existing PRs. But it works differently than the objective envisioned:
- Incremental dispatch = plan-first (you write the plan locally, CI implements) - Objective's vision = prompt-first (you type a sentence, CI plans AND implements)
Key findings: - Node 1.1: Done, landed - Node 1.2 (PR #8593): Stale since Mar 2. The handler sends modify_existing: "true" but the workflow doesn't accept that input — so erk launch one-shot --pr currently runs the full one-shot flow incorrectly - Node 1.3: Superseded by incremental dispatch's local planning approach - Nodes 2.1-2.4 (TUI): Still unique — incremental dispatch has zero TUI presence
I've written up three options (close, narrow to TUI, or fix the prompt-driven path) in the plan file.
● User answered Claude's questions: ⎿ · Which direction do you want to take with objective #8470? → Narrow to TUI only
● Good choice. Let me update the plan for the "Narrow to TUI" direction.
● Updated plan ⎿ /plan to preview
● Let me verify one thing before finalizing — whether the one-shot launch path has tests or other references that would need cleanup.
● Searched for 2 patterns (ctrl+o to expand)
● There are quite a few one-shot test files — removing the entire erk launch one-shot path would be a bigger surgery than warranted. Let me simplify the plan to just the essential changes.
● Updated plan ⎿ /plan to preview
● Here's the plan:
--- Plan: Narrow Objective #8470 to TUI-Only Scope
Context
Objective #8470 ("One-Shot Modification of Existing PRs") is partially superseded by incremental dispatch (#8785, #8789, #8850, #8853), which provides plan-first modification of existing PRs via CLI. The prompt-driven CI path (nodes 1.2, 1.3) is no longer needed. We're narrowing the objective to TUI integration of incremental dispatch.
Changes
1. Close stale PR #8593
Close with a comment: "Superseded by incremental dispatch (#8785). The modify_existing workflow path is no longer needed — users can use /erk:pr-incremental-dispatch instead."
2. Remove dead modify_existing input from launch_cmd.py
File: src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py:334
Remove line "modify_existing": "true" from the workflow inputs dict in _dispatch_one_shot(). This input is silently ignored by one-shot.yml (never declared as an input). The rest of the function stays — erk launch one-shot --pr still works as a "plan-and-implement from prompt against existing PR branch" path, it just doesn't have the modify-existing semantics the objective envisioned.
3. Update objective issue #8470
Post a progress comment and update the roadmap YAML:
Roadmap changes: - Node 1.2 → status: dropped, description updated: "Superseded by incremental dispatch (#8785)" - Node 1.3 → status: dropped, description updated: "Superseded by incremental dispatch (#8785)" - Node 2.1 → rename slug to plan-input-screen, update description: "Create PlanInputScreen modal for writing/pasting incremental dispatch plans" - Node 2.2 → rename slug to incremental-dispatch-command, update description: "Add incremental_dispatch CommandDefinition to TUI registry" - Node 2.3 → update description: "Wire incremental dispatch (erk exec incremental-dispatch) into TUI async worker" - Node 2.4 → update description: "Add incremental dispatch handler in plan_detail_screen.py"
Objective body update: Revise to describe TUI-first incremental dispatch rather than prompt-driven one-shot modification.
Files Modified
- src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py — Remove dead modify_existing line (1 line change) - GitHub PR #8593 — Closed - GitHub issue #8470 — Updated roadmap and objective body
Verification
1. Run fast-ci to confirm no test breakage from the 1-line removal 2. Verify erk launch one-shot --pr still works (just without the dead input) 3. Check objective issue #8470 shows updated roadmap
---
● User answered Claude's questions: ⎿ · 📋 Plan: Narrow Objective #8470 to TUI-Only Scope Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)

Remove line "modify_existing": "true" from the workflow inputs dict in _dispatch_one_shot(). This input is silently ignored by one-shot.yml (never declared as an input). The rest of the function stays — erk launch one-shot --pr still works as a "plan-and-implement from prompt against existing PR branch" path, it just doesn't have the modify-existing semantics the objective envisioned.

● Good, nodes 1.2 and 1.3 are marked as skipped. Now I need to add a new node for the modify_existing cleanup and update the 2.x node descriptions. Let me check if I can add nodes programmatically, or if I need to edit the comment body directly.
● Bash(erk exec --help 2>&1 | grep -i "objective\|node\|roadmap" | head -20) ⎿ get-plans-for-objective Fetch erk-plans linked to an objective. migrate-objective-schema Migrate an objective's roadmap YAML to... objective-apply-landed-update Apply mechanical updates to an... … +9 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
● There's no "add node" command. Let me post a comprehensive progress comment on the objective documenting the evaluation and the new scope, including the modify_existing cleanup as work to be done.
● Bash(gh issue comment 8470 --repo dagster-io/erk --body "$(cat <<'EOF' ## Action: Objective Re-evaluation — Narrowing to TUI-Only Scope…) ⎿ https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/issues/8470#issuecomment-4015167744
● Done. Here's what I did:
1. Closed PR #8593 — stale since Mar 2, superseded by incremental dispatch 2. Marked nodes 1.2 and 1.3 as skipped in the objective roadmap 3. Posted a progress comment on #8470 documenting: - The evaluation findings (incremental dispatch supersedes the CI modification path) - The modify_existing dead code cleanup needed in launch_cmd.py:334 (noted for future work, not edited now) - Revised Phase 2 node descriptions targeting incremental dispatch in the TUI
The objective is now scoped to TUI integration only. The modify_existing cleanup is documented in the comment as work to be done alongside the TUI nodes.
✻ Cooked for 1m 25s
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ▪▪▪ ─ ❯ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)
how can we improve things here ? i'm noting the "add node" message
session.assistant:
Good observation. Here's what I hit during this workflow:
**Gaps in objective node management:**
1. **No `add-objective-node`** — I couldn't programmatically add a new node (e.g., for the modify_existing cleanup). Had to document it in a comment instead, meaning it's not in the structured roadmap YAML.
2. **No `--description` or `--slug` on `update-objective-node`** — Could only change status/PR fields. Couldn't update node descriptions to reflect the new TUI-focused scope (e.g., renaming "modify-existing-workflow-input" to something about incremental dispatch).
3. **No `--reason` on status changes** — Marking 1.2/1.3 as "skipped" loses the *why* (superseded by #8785). That context only lives in the free-form comment I posted.
**The practical impact:** The objective's roadmap YAML is now inconsistent — nodes 1.2/1.3 are marked skipped but still have their old descriptions, and the modify_existing cleanup isn't a real node. Anyone reading the roadmap table gets a stale picture.
**Possible improvements, roughly ordered by value:**
- `update-objective-node --description "..."` — cheapest win, lets you update text on existing nodes - `add-objective-node --phase 1 --slug cleanup-modify-existing --description "..."` — add nodes without manual YAML surgery - `update-objective-node --status skipped --reason "Superseded by #8785"` — structured skip reasons
Want me to turn any of these into a plan?
session.user:
- update-objective-node --description "..." — cheapest win, lets you update text on existing nodes - add-objective-node --phase 1 --slug cleanup-modify-existing --description "..." — add nodes without manual YAML surgery - update-objective-node --status skipped --reason "Superseded by #8785" — structured skip reasons
----------
let's add these and also add it to the objective skill

session.tool_result:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the current objective skill and documentation. Let me create a summary report for you.
## Summary: Objective Skill Documentation Analysis
I've found and thoroughly reviewed the objective skill file and related documentation. Here's what currently exists:
### Current Skill File Location - **Main skill**: `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.claude/skills/objective/SKILL.md` (5,206 bytes) - **References**: - `format.md` - Complete templates and update patterns (15,685 bytes) - `workflow.md` - Detailed workflow procedures (7,208 bytes) - `updating.md` - Quick reference for two-step updates (1,359 bytes) - `closing.md` - Closing triggers and procedures (3,391 bytes)
### Current Capabilities Documented
**Core Operations:** 1. **Creating objectives** - Manual or assisted creation workflow 2. **Viewing objectives** - Using `erk objective view` or `/local:objective-view` 3. **Logging actions** - Posting action comments with structured format 4. **Updating the body** - Roadmap status, reconciliation of stale prose 5. **Spawning erk-plans** - Creating plans for individual roadmap nodes 6. **Closing objectives** - When work is complete or abandoned
**Node Update Capabilities (current):** - Update PR links: `--pr` flag in `update-objective-node` - Update status: `--status` flag (pending, in-progress, done, blocked, skipped) - Multiple node updates in single command: `--node` can be used multiple times
**Roadmap Statuses:**
... [58 lines omitted] ...

Perfect! I now have enough comprehensive information to provide a detailed report. Let me compile my findings.
## Summary
I've thoroughly explored erk's objective node management tooling. Here's a comprehensive understanding of the system:
---
## 1. **Update Objective Node Exec Script**
**Location:** `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py`
This is the primary command for updating roadmap nodes. Key features:
- **CLI Interface:** `erk exec update-objective-node <ISSUE_NUMBER> --node <NODE_ID> --pr <PR_REF> [--status <STATUS>] [--include-body]` - **Parameters:** - `--node` (multiple, required): Node IDs to update (e.g., `1.3`, `5.1`) - `--pr`: PR reference (`#123`, `plan #456`, or empty string to clear) - `--status`: Explicit status (`pending`, `planning`, `in_progress`, `done`, `blocked`, `skipped`) - `--include-body`: Returns the fully updated issue body in output
- **What it does:** 1. Fetches the issue and parses the roadmap to validate nodes exist 2. For each node, finds current PR value, replaces it in the frontmatter YAML 3. Computes status: explicit `--status` takes priority; otherwise infers from PR column 4. Updates issue body (single API call for all nodes) 5. Re-renders the markdown comment table from the updated YAML
- **Output:** Structured JSON with `{success, issue_number, node_id, previous_pr, new_pr, url}` or error codes (all exit with code 0, check JSON for pass/fail)
... [216 lines omitted] ...
| `parse_roadmap(body)` | Parses frontmatter, returns `(phases, validation_errors)` | | `validate_roadmap_frontmatter(data)` | YAML validation, returns `(nodes, errors)` |
session.assistant: Let me read the key implementation files before finalizing the plan.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Update node PR cells in an objective's roadmap table. 2→ 3→Why this command exists (instead of using update-issue-body directly): 4→ 5→ The alternative is "fetch body -> parse markdown table -> find node row -> 6→ surgically edit the PR cell -> write entire body back". That's ~15 7→ lines of fragile ad-hoc Python that every caller (skills, hooks, scripts) 8→ must duplicate. The roadmap table has a specific structure 9→ (| node_id | description | status | pr |) and the update has 10→ specific semantics: 11→ 12→ 1. Find the row by node ID across all phases 13→ 2. Compute display status from the PR value 14→ 3. Write status and PR cells atomically so the table is 15→ always human-readable without requiring a parse pass 16→ 17→ Encoding this once in a tested CLI command means: 18→ - No duplicated table-parsing logic across callers 19→ - Testable edge cases (node not found, no roadmap, clearing PR) 20→ - Atomic mental model: "update node 1.3's PR to X" 21→ - Resilient to roadmap format changes (one command updates, not N sites) 22→ 23→Usage: 24→ # Single node -- landed PR 25→ erk exec update-objective-node 6423 --node 1.3 --pr "#6500" --status done 26→ 27→ # Clear PR 28→ erk exec update-objective-node 6423 --node 1.3 --pr "" 29→ 30→ # Status-only update (preserve existing PR)
... [339 lines omitted] ...
41→ Each node result: {node_id, success, previous_pr, error} 70→def _node_error_message(node_id: str, issue_number: int, error: object) -> str: 71→ if error == "node_not_found": 73→ return f"Failed to replace cells for node '{node_id}'" 105→ "error": single_result["error"], 106→ "message": _node_error_message(node[0], issue_number, single_result["error"]), 183→ except ValueError: 230→ "error": "no_update", 247→ "error": "issue_not_found", 261→ "error": "no_roadmap", 273→ {"node_id": n, "success": False, "error": "node_not_found"} for n in missing_nodes 290→ any_failure = False 299→ "error": "node_not_found", 302→ any_failure = True 316→ "error": "replacement_failed", 319→ any_failure = True 331→ # Exit early if all nodes failed 332→ if any_failure and not any(r["success"] for r in results):

1→"""Shared parser, serializer, and data types for objective roadmap operations. 2→ 3→This module provides: 4→- Data types: RoadmapNodeStatus, RoadmapNode, RoadmapPhase 5→- Parsing: parse_roadmap() (v2 frontmatter only) 6→- Frontmatter: validate, parse, group, update 7→- Utilities: compute_summary(), find_next_node(), serialize_phases() 8→ 9→Previously split across objective_roadmap_shared.py and 10→objective_roadmap_frontmatter.py in the erk package. Consolidated 11→here to eliminate the circular dependency between erk_shared and erk. 12→""" 13→ 14→import re 15→from collections.abc import Mapping 16→from dataclasses import dataclass, replace 17→from typing import Literal, cast 18→ 19→import yaml 20→ 21→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import ( 22→ extract_raw_metadata_blocks, 23→ parse_metadata_block_body, 24→) 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys 26→ 27→RoadmapNodeStatus = Literal["pending", "planning", "done", "in_progress", "blocked", "skipped"] 28→ 29→ 30→@dataclass(frozen=True)
... [726 lines omitted] ...
66→ Tuple of (steps, errors). If validation succeeds, 67→ errors is empty. If validation fails, steps is None. 69→ errors: list[str] = [] 74→ errors.append("Missing required field: schema_version") 75→ return None, errors 78→ errors.append(f"Unsupported schema_version: {schema_version}") 79→ return None, errors 87→ errors.append("Missing required field: nodes (or steps for v2)") 88→ return None, errors 90→ errors.append("Field 'steps' must be a list") 91→ return None, errors 97→ errors.append(f"Step {i} is not a mapping") 98→ return None, errors 105→ errors.append(f"Step {i} missing required field: {field}") 106→ return None, errors 118→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'id' must be a string") 119→ return None, errors 121→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'description' must be a string") 122→ return None, errors 124→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'status' must be a string") 125→ return None, errors 128→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'status' must be one of: {valid_statuses}") 129→ return None, errors 131→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'pr' must be a string or null") 132→ return None, errors 138→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'depends_on' must be a list or null") 139→ return None, errors 142→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'depends_on' item {j} must be a string") 143→ return None, errors 148→ errors.append(f"Step {i} field 'slug' must be a string or null") 149→ return None, errors 162→ return steps, errors 182→ except ValueError: 184→ steps, _errors = validate_roadmap_frontmatter(data) 191→ "<!-- WARNING: Machine-generated. Manual edits may break erk tooling. -->\n" 423→ ``(phases, validation_errors)`` on success, or ``None`` when the 442→ steps, errors = validate_roadmap_frontmatter(data) 448→ return (phases, errors) 451→_LEGACY_FORMAT_ERROR = ( 462→ (phases, validation_errors) 480→ # Block exists but failed to parse — legacy/broken format 481→ return ([], [_LEGACY_FORMAT_ERROR])

1→--- 2→name: objective 3→description: > 4→ This skill should be used when managing objectives - coherent goals that require 2+ plans/PRs 5→ to complete. Use when users want to create multi-plan tracking documents, log progress across 6→ related PRs, capture lessons learned, or coordinate work that spans multiple erk-plans. 7→ Objectives are human-first coordination documents, not executable plans. 8→--- 9→ 10→# Objective Skill 11→ 12→## Overview 13→ 14→Objectives are coordination documents for goals requiring multiple plans/PRs to complete. Unlike 15→erk-plans (single executable implementations), objectives track progress across related work and 16→capture lessons learned along the way. 17→ 18→**Scope range:** 19→ 20→- Small: Feature requiring 2-3 related PRs 21→- Medium: Refactor spanning several plans 22→- Large: Long-running strategic direction emitting many plans 23→ 24→## Objective vs Erk-Plan 25→ 26→| Aspect | Erk-Plan | Objective | 27→| -------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | 28→| Purpose | Single executable implementation | Coordinate 2+ related plans/PRs | 29→| Scope | One PR or tightly-coupled change | Multiple plans toward coherent goal | 30→| Body | Machine-parseable metadata | Human-readable markdown |
... [120 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Unit tests for update-objective-node command.""" 2→ 3→import json 4→from datetime import UTC, datetime 5→ 6→from click.testing import CliRunner 7→ 8→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.update_objective_node import update_objective_node 9→from erk_shared.context.context import ErkContext 10→from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub 11→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues 12→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueComment, IssueInfo 13→ 14→ROADMAP_BODY_V2 = """\ 15→# Objective: Build Feature X 16→ 17→ 18→ 19→
20→objective-roadmap 21→ 22→```yaml 23→ 24→schema_version: '2' 25→steps: 26→ - id: '1.1' 27→ description: Set up project structure 28→ status: done 29→ pr: '#100' 30→ - id: '1.2' ... [738 lines omitted] ... 106→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 134→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 145→ """Error when step ID doesn't exist in roadmap.""" 159→ assert output["error"] == "node_not_found" 164→ """Error when issue doesn't exist.""" 177→ assert output["error"] == "issue_not_found" 182→ """Error when issue has no roadmap table.""" 196→ assert output["error"] == "no_roadmap" 211→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 234→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 277→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 297→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 318→def test_update_multiple_steps_partial_failure() -> None: 346→ replacement_failed path is defensive (parse_roadmap and 354→ {"node_id": "1.3", "success": False, "error": "replacement_failed"}, 366→ # AND semantics: success=false because step 1.3 failed 386→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 410→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 433→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 453→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 473→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 517→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 526→def test_include_body_on_failure() -> None: 527→ """updated_body field is absent on failure even with --include-body.""" 545→ """When issue body has no metadata block, returns no_roadmap error.""" 570→ assert output["error"] == "no_roadmap" 576→ 592→ 621→ 666→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 716→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 738→ assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Failed: {result.output}" 751→def test_neither_pr_nor_status_returns_error() -> None: 752→ """Omitting both --pr and --status returns a no_update error.""" 766→ assert output["error"] == "no_update"

PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/shimmering-gathering-wand.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Objective Node Management Improvements Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 2af512ee-1fb0-4260-9130-037373da7bfe \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.
... [8 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Let me display the plan and present the options.
session.tool_result:
1→# Plan: Objective Node Management Improvements 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→During objective #8470 re-evaluation, we hit gaps in node management tooling: 6→- Couldn't update node descriptions after the objective scope changed 7→- Couldn't add new nodes programmatically (had to document in comments instead) 8→- Couldn't attach reasons when skipping nodes (lost the "why") 9→ 10→Three improvements, all building on the existing `update-objective-node` patterns. 11→ 12→## Changes 13→ 14→### 1. Add `--description`, `--slug`, `--reason` to `update-objective-node` 15→ 16→**Files:** 17→- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py` — RoadmapNode + frontmatter functions 18→- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_objective_node.py` — CLI command 19→- `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_objective_node.py` — tests 20→ 21→**roadmap.py changes:** 22→- Add `reason: str | None` field to `RoadmapNode` (after `slug`, default `None`) 23→- Update `validate_roadmap_frontmatter()`: parse optional `reason` field from YAML 24→- Update `render_roadmap_block_inner()`: include `reason` in YAML output only when non-None 25→- Extend `update_node_in_frontmatter()` signature: add `description: str | None = None`, `slug: str | None = None`, `reason: str | None = None` kwargs. Apply via `replace()` when non-None, same pattern as existing `pr`/`status`. 26→- `reason` is NOT shown in rendered markdown tables (display metadata only, visible in raw YAML) 27→ 28→**update_objective_node.py changes:** 29→- Add Click options: `--description`, `--slug` (strings), `--reason` (string) 30→- Update validation: at least one of `--pr`, `--status`, `--description`, `--slug`, `--reason` required
... [92 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Plan: Objective Node Management Improvements\nCurrent context: (wt:erk) (...[truncated 395 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Plan: Objective Node Management Improvements Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
Launching skill: erk:plan-save
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="2af512ee-1fb0-4260-9130-037373da7bfe" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="2af512ee-1fb0-4260-9130-037373da7bfe" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "2af512ee-1fb0-4260-9130-037373da7bfe" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "2af512ee-1fb0-4260-9130-037373da7bfe" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "2af512ee-1fb0-4260-9130-037373da7bfe" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "2af512ee-1fb0-4260-9130-037373da7bfe" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
Let me execute the plan-save workflow.
**Branch slug:** `add-objective-node-mgmt`
**Plan summary:** Adds three capabilities to objective node management: updating node descriptions/slugs/reasons on existing nodes, adding new nodes programmatically via add-objective-node, and attaching structured skip reasons. Also updates the objective skill documentation.

tool_use.name: Bash

tool_use.name: ExitPlanMode
tool_use.param:

## Context
During objective #8470 re-eval...[truncated 5247 chars]...against objective #8470: add the modify_existing cleanup node and update 2.x descriptions

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: Plan #8886 saved successfully.
Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):
Implement plan #8886: In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8886 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8886 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8886 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8886 --script)" && erk implement -d
Checkout plan #8886: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8886 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8886
Dispatch plan #8886: CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8886 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch
Session complete. Do NOT call ExitPlanMode again.
session.assistant:
Plan #8886 saved successfully.
**Implement plan #8886:** ``` In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8886 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8886 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8886 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8886 --script)" && erk implement -d ```
**Checkout plan #8886:** ``` In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8886 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8886 ```
**Dispatch plan #8886:** ``` CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8886 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch ```
session.user:
# /erk:pr-dispatch
## Goal
Find the most recent plan created in this conversation and dispatch it for remote AI implementation via `erk pr dispatch`.
## What This Command Does
1. Search conversation for the last plan reference 2. Extract the plan number 3. Run `erk pr dispatch <plan_number>` to trigger remote implementation
## Finding the Plan
Search the conversation from bottom to top for these patterns (in priority order):
1. **Draft PR reference**: `saved as draft PR #<number>` or `draft PR #<number>` 2. **Pull request URL**: `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<number>` 3. **Plan URL**: `https://github.com/.../pull/<number>` (legacy: `https://github.com/.../issues/<number>`)
Extract the plan number from the most recent match.
## Execution
Once you have the plan number, run:
```bash erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If the command succeeds, clear the session marker to allow creating new plans in this session:
```bash erk exec marker delete --session-id "2af512ee-1fb0-4260-9130-037373da7bfe" plan-saved-issue ```
Display the command output to the user. The `erk pr dispatch` command handles all validation (plan existence, labels, state).
## Error Cases
- **No plan found in conversation**: Report "No plan found in conversation. Run /erk:plan-save first to create a plan." - **erk pr dispatch fails**: Display the error output from the command (erk pr dispatch validates the plan)
session.assistant: The most recent plan is #8886. Let me dispatch it.

Dispatching workflow: plan-implement.yml Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) ✓ Workflow dispatched. ✓ Dispatch metadata written Posting queued event comment... ✓ Queued event comment posted
✓ 1 plan(s) dispatched successfully!
Dispatched plans:

{"success": true, "message": "Deleted marker: plan-saved-issue"}
session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
